info.name: Fonderie.js
info.description:
API reference for **Fonderie.js** — a modular TypeScript framework that ships the first three months of every SaaS already built.

Each folder maps directly to an `@fonderie/*` package. Register only the modules you need — the framework is intentionally composable.

---

**Base URL:** `{{base_url}}` (default `http://localhost:4000/v1`)

**Authentication:** Protected endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`. The `token` variable is auto-captured after login or register.

**Response envelope:** Every application response follows a consistent shape:
```json
{
  "reason": "SNAKE_CASE_CODE",
  "explanation": "Human-readable description.",
  "result": { ... }
}
```
Error responses omit `result` and use a `4xx` / `5xx` status code.

**Collection variables (auto-captured):**
| Variable | Captured by | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `base_url` | — | Server base URL |
| `token` | Login / Register | Short-lived access JWT |
| `refresh_token` | Login / Register | Long-lived refresh token |
| `workspace_id` | Create Workspace | Active workspace UUID |
| `plan_id` | List Plans | First plan UUID from catalogue |

item.name: Auth  (@fonderie/auth)
Registration & Login

request.description: Create a new user account. Returns access + refresh tokens. Email verification is required before accessing protected endpoints.

response.name: 201 Created

request.description:
Authenticate with email + password. Returns access + refresh tokens.

If MFA is enabled the response will contain `mfaPending: true` and a short-lived token — submit the TOTP code to `POST /auth/mfa/verify` to get a full token.

item.description: Core credential flows. None of these endpoints require an `Authorization` header. Tokens returned by login and register are automatically captured into the `{{token}}` and `{{refresh_token}}` collection variables.

item.name: Email Verification

request.description: Send a verification OTP to the authenticated user's email or phone depending on their login method. Rate-limited per account.

originalRequest.description: Send a verification OTP to the authenticated user's email or phone depending on their login method. Rate-limited per account.
Send a verification OTP to the authenticated user's email or phone depending on their login method. Rate-limited per account.

request.description:
Submit the 6-digit OTP received by email. Marks the account as verified.

For phone sessions, use the **Phone Auth → Verify Phone OTP** request instead — phone verification returns a new token pair.

originalRequest.description:
Submit the 6-digit OTP received by email. Marks the account as verified.

For phone sessions, use the **Phone Auth → Verify Phone OTP** request instead — phone verification returns a new token pair.

Submit the 6-digit OTP received by email. Marks the account as verified.

For phone sessions, use the **Phone Auth → Verify Phone OTP** request instead — phone verification returns a new token pair.

item.description:
After registration the user's email is unverified (`isEmailVerified: false`). Several endpoints — profile updates, email/phone changes, account deletion — require a verified email.

The OTP is delivered to email or phone depending on the user's login method. Verification requests are rate-limited; re-sending too quickly returns `429`.

item.name: Password Reset

request.description: Request a password reset email. Always returns `ok: true` to prevent email enumeration.

response.name: 200 OK

originalRequest.description: Request a password reset email. Always returns `ok: true` to prevent email enumeration.

request.description: Set a new password using the 6-digit PIN received by email. The PIN is single-use and expires after 1 hour.

originalRequest.description: Set a new password using the 6-digit PIN received by email. The PIN is single-use and expires after 1 hour.

response.name: 400 Invalid or expired token

originalRequest.description: Set a new password using the 6-digit PIN received by email. The PIN is single-use and expires after 1 hour.

item.description:
Self-service password reset via email. Two-step flow:
1. `POST /auth/email/forgot` — user submits their email and receives a reset link
2. `POST /auth/email/reset` — user submits the token from the link and their new password

`/forgot` always returns `ok: true` regardless of whether the email exists — this prevents email enumeration. The reset token is single-use and expires after a short window.

item.name: Google OAuth

request.description:
Returns a Google authorization URL. Redirect the user's browser to this URL to begin the OAuth flow.

Requires `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` to be set in the server environment.

response.name: 200 OK

originalRequest.description:
Returns a Google authorization URL. Redirect the user's browser to this URL to begin the OAuth flow.

Requires `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` to be set in the server environment.

request.description: Handled automatically by Google after the user authorises. Creates or updates the user account and returns tokens. Not called directly in production — included here for testing.

originalRequest.description: Handled automatically by Google after the user authorises. Creates or updates the user account and returns tokens. Not called directly in production — included here for testing.

item.description:
Server-side Google OAuth 2.0 flow. Only registered when `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` are set in the server environment.

**Flow:**
1. Frontend calls `GET /auth/google` → receives an authorization URL
2. Redirect the user's browser to that URL
3. Google redirects back to `GET /auth/google/callback?code=...`
4. Server exchanges the code for a Google profile, upserts the user account, and returns the standard access + refresh token pair

The callback URL defaults to `http://127.0.0.1:4000/v1/auth/google/callback` and must match the redirect URI registered in your Google Cloud console.

item.name: Phone Auth

request.description:
Create a new account using a phone number. An OTP is sent to the provided number immediately.

Returns an unverified token pair (status 202). The account must be confirmed via `POST /auth/verify` before accessing endpoints that require a verified session.

`firstName` and `lastName` are optional.

response.name: 202 Accepted

originalRequest.description:
Create a new account using a phone number. An OTP is sent to the provided number immediately.

Returns an unverified token pair (status 202). The account must be confirmed via `POST /auth/verify` before accessing endpoints that require a verified session.

`firstName` and `lastName` are optional.

Create a new account using a phone number. An OTP is sent to the provided number immediately.

Returns an unverified token pair (status 202). The account must be confirmed via `POST /auth/verify` before accessing endpoints that require a verified session.

`firstName` and `lastName` are optional.

request.description:
Request an OTP for an existing phone account. Returns an unverified token pair immediately (status 202) — this token is used to authenticate the follow-up `POST /auth/verify` call.

The session is not considered verified until the OTP is confirmed.

response.name: 202 OTP sent

The session is not considered verified until the OTP is confirmed.

The session is not considered verified until the OTP is confirmed.

Request an OTP for an existing phone account. Returns an unverified token pair immediately (status 202) — this token is used to authenticate the follow-up `POST /auth/verify` call.

The session is not considered verified until the OTP is confirmed.

request.description:
Submit the 6-digit OTP received by SMS. On success, issues a **new verified token pair** which replaces the pre-verification tokens. The `{{token}}` collection variable is updated automatically.

Use the unverified `token` returned by Register or Login as the `Authorization: Bearer` header for this call.

originalRequest.description:
Submit the 6-digit OTP received by SMS. On success, issues a **new verified token pair** which replaces the pre-verification tokens. The `{{token}}` collection variable is updated automatically.

Use the unverified `token` returned by Register or Login as the `Authorization: Bearer` header for this call.

Submit the 6-digit OTP received by SMS. On success, issues a **new verified token pair** which replaces the pre-verification tokens. The `{{token}}` collection variable is updated automatically.

Use the unverified `token` returned by Register or Login as the `Authorization: Bearer` header for this call.

item.description:
Passwordless phone authentication via SMS OTP. Available when `phone` is listed in `providers` in `IAuthConfig`.

**Register flow:**
1. `POST /auth/register` with `{ phone }` → creates account, sends OTP, returns a pre-verified token pair (status 202)
2. `POST /auth/verify` with `{ pin }` + the token from step 1 → confirms the phone number and issues a verified token pair

**Login flow (returning user):**
1. `POST /auth/login` with `{ phone }` → sends OTP, returns an unverified token pair (status 202)
2. `POST /auth/verify` with `{ pin }` + that token → issues a verified token pair

MFA is not available for phone sessions — the OTP itself is the second factor.

item.name: MFA

request.description:
Initiate MFA setup. Returns a base64 QR code PNG (`qr`) and a set of one-time backup codes. The TOTP secret is in `pending` state until confirmed with `POST /auth/mfa/verify`.

Store the backup codes immediately — they are only shown once.

Requires: authenticated + verified email + email login method.

response.name: 200 OK

originalRequest.description:
Initiate MFA setup. Returns a base64 QR code PNG (`qr`) and a set of one-time backup codes. The TOTP secret is in `pending` state until confirmed with `POST /auth/mfa/verify`.

Store the backup codes immediately — they are only shown once.

Requires: authenticated + verified email + email login method.

request.description:
Confirm a TOTP `token` from your authenticator app.

**Two contexts:**
- **Setup confirmation** (when `pendingSecret` exists): activates MFA, returns `{ mfaEnabled: true }`
- **Login completion** (when called with the `mfaToken` from a login response): issues a full access + refresh token pair

Backup codes (8-character uppercase strings) can be submitted as `token` in place of a TOTP code and will be consumed on use.

Use the `mfaToken` from the login response as the `Authorization: Bearer` token for this call.

originalRequest.description:
Confirm a TOTP `token` from your authenticator app.

**Two contexts:**
- **Setup confirmation** (when `pendingSecret` exists): activates MFA, returns `{ mfaEnabled: true }`
- **Login completion** (when called with the `mfaToken` from a login response): issues a full access + refresh token pair

Backup codes (8-character uppercase strings) can be submitted as `token` in place of a TOTP code and will be consumed on use.

Use the `mfaToken` from the login response as the `Authorization: Bearer` token for this call.

originalRequest.description: Disable MFA for the current account. Requires a valid TOTP code or backup code to confirm intent.
Disable MFA for the current account. Requires a valid TOTP code or backup code to confirm intent.

request.description: Invalidate existing backup codes and generate a new set. Requires a valid TOTP code.

originalRequest.description: Invalidate existing backup codes and generate a new set. Requires a valid TOTP code.
Invalidate existing backup codes and generate a new set. Requires a valid TOTP code.
Invalidate existing backup codes and generate a new set. Requires a valid TOTP code.

item.description:
TOTP-based multi-factor authentication (Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password, etc.). Only available for email login sessions with a verified email.

**Setup flow:**
1. `POST /auth/mfa/setup` → returns a QR code image (base64 PNG) and the raw TOTP secret
2. User scans the QR code in their authenticator app
3. `POST /auth/mfa/verify` with the first code → activates MFA and returns one-time backup codes

**Login flow (once MFA is enabled):**
1. `POST /auth/login` returns `{ mfaPending: true, accessToken: "<short-lived token>" }` instead of a full session
2. `POST /auth/mfa/verify` with the TOTP code → exchanges the pending token for full access + refresh tokens

Backup codes can be used in place of a TOTP code everywhere a code is accepted.

request.description:
Update the account email address. Requires verified email.

Side effects:
- Sends a verification code to the **new** email
- Sends a change-alert to the **old** email
- Marks the account as unverified until the new address is confirmed

response.name: 200 OK

originalRequest.description:
Update the account email address. Requires verified email.

Side effects:
- Sends a verification code to the **new** email
- Sends a change-alert to the **old** email

request.description:
Update the account phone number. Requires verified email.

Side effects:
- Sends an OTP to the **new** phone number
- Sends a change-alert email to the account email address

response.name: 200 OK

originalRequest.description:
Update the account phone number. Requires verified email.

Side effects:

- Sends a change-alert email to the account email address

request.description: Permanently delete the authenticated user's account. Requires verified email. This action is irreversible.

originalRequest.description: Permanently delete the authenticated user's account. Requires verified email. This action is irreversible.

item.description:
Self-service profile management for the authenticated user. All endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`.

Endpoints that mutate identity-sensitive fields (`/profile`, `/preferences`, `/email`, `/phone`, `DELETE /users`) additionally require a verified email (`isEmailVerified: true`). Changing email or phone triggers a new verification flow before the change takes effect.

All authentication, session, and identity management — provided by `@fonderie/auth`.

**Three supported login methods:**
- **Email + password** — register, login, forgot/reset password, TOTP MFA
- **Phone + OTP** — login via SMS one-time code
- **Google OAuth** — one-click sign-in (requires `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` + `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` env vars)

All methods return the same pair: `accessToken` (short-lived JWT) and `refreshToken` (long-lived, stored server-side and invalidated on logout).

**Email verification** is a separate step after registration. Several downstream endpoints (profile edits, email/phone changes, account deletion) require `isEmailVerified: true`.

item.name: Billing  (@fonderie/billing)
Plans

request.description: Return all active plans. No authentication required — safe to call from marketing pages or mobile apps before sign-up.

response.name: 200 OK

originalRequest.description: Return all active plans. No authentication required — safe to call from marketing pages or mobile apps before sign-up.

request.description: Return a single plan by its UUID.

originalRequest.description: Return a single plan by its UUID.
Return a single plan by its UUID.

request.description: Create a new billing plan. Admin use only.
Update an existing billing plan. Admin use only.
Delete a billing plan. Admin use only.

item.description:
Read-only plan catalogue. No authentication required — safe to call from a marketing page or pricing component before the user has signed up.

Plans are **config-driven**: defined in `IBillingConfig.plans` and synced to the database on every server boot via `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE`. There are no write endpoints — to add, rename, or reprice a plan, change the config and redeploy.

request.description: Return the total usage for a given metric since the start of the current billing period.

originalRequest.description: Return the total usage for a given metric since the start of the current billing period.

item.description:
Subscription scoped to the authenticated user (`subscriberType: 'user'`). No workspace context required — suitable for consumer/individual SaaS (Spotify, Netflix-style).

The subscriber is always resolved as the user in the access token. No `X-Workspace-ID` header is needed.

item.name: Workspace-scoped  (X-Workspace-ID)

request.description: Return the active subscription for the workspace identified by `X-Workspace-ID`.

originalRequest.description: Return the active subscription for the workspace identified by `X-Workspace-ID`.

request.description: Create a Stripe checkout session for the workspace identified by `X-Workspace-ID`.

originalRequest.description: Create a Stripe checkout session for the workspace identified by `X-Workspace-ID`.

request.description: Generate a Stripe billing portal URL for the workspace subscription.

originalRequest.description: Generate a Stripe billing portal URL for the workspace subscription.

request.description: Record a usage event against the workspace subscription.

originalRequest.description: Record a usage event against the workspace subscription.

request.description: Return total usage for a metric for this workspace since the start of the billing period.

originalRequest.description: Return total usage for a metric for this workspace since the start of the billing period.

item.description:
Same `/billing/*` endpoints as user billing — the subscriber is switched to the workspace by adding the `X-Workspace-ID` header. Membership is verified automatically by the `withBilling` global middleware; no separate guard needed.

Suitable for team / B2B SaaS (Slack, Linear-style).

item.name: Webhook

request.description:
Stripe webhook receiver. The signature is verified internally using `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.

Handled events:
- `customer.subscription.created`
- `customer.subscription.updated`
- `customer.subscription.deleted`

All other event types return `{ received: true }` without side effects.

originalRequest.description:
Stripe webhook receiver. The signature is verified internally using `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.

Handled events:
- `customer.subscription.created`
- `customer.subscription.updated`
- `customer.subscription.deleted`

All other event types return `{ received: true }` without side effects.

Stripe webhook receiver. The signature is verified internally using `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.

Handled events:
- `customer.subscription.created`
- `customer.subscription.updated`
- `customer.subscription.deleted`

All other event types return `{ received: true }` without side effects.

item.description:
Stripe webhook receiver — called by Stripe, not by your frontend. Configure this URL in your Stripe Dashboard under **Developers → Webhooks**.

The `stripe-signature` header is verified against `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Requests with an invalid or missing signature are rejected with `400`.

**Handled events:**
- `customer.subscription.created` — creates the subscription record
- `customer.subscription.updated` — syncs status, billing period, and cancel-at-period-end flag
- `customer.subscription.deleted` — marks the subscription as cancelled

All other event types return `{ received: true }` without side effects.

Subscription billing and usage metering — provided by `@fonderie/billing`.

**Subscriber resolution** — the same `/billing/*` routes serve both user and workspace billing. The module resolves the subscriber automatically:

| Header present | Resolved as |
|---|---|
| `X-Workspace-ID: <id>` | `subscriberType: 'workspace'` — membership verified automatically |
| *(omitted)* | `subscriberType: 'user'` — the authenticated user |

**Plan catalogue** is config-driven: plans live in `IBillingConfig.plans`, are synced to the database on every boot, and exposed as read-only via the API.

**Amounts** are in cents (e.g. `7900` = $79.00). `null` limit = unlimited.

query.description: Skip family resolution; returns shallow tenant data only (12 queries vs 18).

request.description:
Retrieve a customer with full detail.

Default returns depth=2: each relationship entry includes its own relationships array (e.g. a tenant's wife, kids, partner). Pass ?depth=1 to skip the second-level resolution and return shallow tenant data only.

Query cost:
- depth=2 (default): 18 parallel-batched queries
- depth=1: 12 parallel-batched queries
Partial update — only send fields you want to change.

response.name: 200 OK

originalRequest.description: Partial update — only send fields you want to change.

response.body:
{
  "reason": "CUSTOMER_UPDATED",
  "explanation": "Customer updated successfully.",
  "result": {
    "customer": {
      "id": "{{customer_id}}",
      "type": "business",
      "sex": "UNKNOWN",
      "firstName": null,
      "lastName": null,
      "companyName": "Acme Landscaping",
      "avatarUrl": null,
      "locale": "en-US",
      "referenceCode": "CLT-001",
      "isArchived": false,
      "createdBy": "{{user_id}}",
      "createdAt": "2026-05-17T10:00:00.000Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-05-17T10:00:00.000Z"
    }
  }
}

request.description: Hard delete a customer. Sub-resources (emails, phones, addresses, notes, tags, relationships) are deleted in parallel before the customer row is removed. Relationships use a detach strategy — related customers are left intact. Address rows in `fonderie_addresses` are also deleted (the join-table FK cascades in the wrong direction for cleanup, so we delete the base rows directly).

originalRequest.description: Hard delete a customer. Sub-resources (emails, phones, addresses, notes, tags, relationships) are deleted in parallel before the customer row is removed. Relationships use a detach strategy — related customers are left intact. Address rows in `fonderie_addresses` are also deleted (the join-table FK cascades in the wrong direction for cleanup, so we delete the base rows directly).

item.name: Job Schemas

Workspace-scoped FormSchema JSON objects. At most one schema can be active at a time.

The mobile app reads `GET /job-schemas/active` on launch instead of loading from AsyncStorage.

request.description: Delete a schema. Returns 409 if the schema is currently active — deactivate another schema first.

item.description:
Custom RBAC roles scoped to a workspace. Roles group fine-grained permissions (READ / CREATE / UPDATE / DELETE) on named resources.

`POST /workspaces/roles/:roleId/permissions` **replaces** the entire permission set atomically — it is not additive. Send the full desired permission list each time.

To assign roles to a member, use the endpoints in the **Members** sub-folder.

item.description:
Manage the members of a workspace. All endpoints require `X-Workspace-ID: {{workspace_id}}`.

Members can hold multiple roles simultaneously. Their effective permissions are the union (BOOL_OR) of all assigned roles — if any role grants a permission, it is granted.

item.name: Invitations

request.description: Invite one or more users to the workspace by email. Body is an array of `{email, roleId?}` objects — pass a single-element array to invite one person. A transactional email is sent to each address. Omitting `roleId` falls back to the workspace ADMIN role.

request.description: Accept a workspace invitation using the token from the invitation email. No workspace context header required.

originalRequest.description: Accept a workspace invitation using the token from the invitation email. No workspace context header required.

item.description:
Token-based workspace invitations. Sending an invitation triggers a transactional email (via `@fonderie/courier`) containing a time-limited acceptance link.

`POST /workspaces/invitations/accept` does **not** require the `X-Workspace-ID` header — the workspace is resolved from the invitation token itself, so unauthenticated users who just registered can accept an invite directly.

item.description:
Multi-tenant team layer — provided by `@fonderie/workspaces`.

A workspace is a shared organizational context (company, team, project). Each workspace has an owner, a slug, a plan tier, and a custom RBAC structure built on top of `@fonderie/permissions`.

**Workspace context resolution:**
- Header-based routes use `X-Workspace-ID: {{workspace_id}}`
- Path-based routes use `:id` or `:workspaceId` directly in the URL

**Sub-sections:**
- **Members** — list, remove, and manage role assignments for workspace members
- **Invitations** — send token-based email invitations and accept them
- **Roles** — define custom roles and attach CRUD permissions per resource
- **Settings** — per-workspace key-value configuration (branding, feature toggles)

item.description:
Cross-cutting utility endpoints available in every Fonderie.js application.

- **Health check** — liveness probe for load balancers and uptime monitors. Returns `503` when `maintenance.mode` is `true` in remote config.
- **Remote config** — inspect all active feature flags and runtime config values. Restricted to non-production environments.
